rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Monetary Sanction Against Deft Rh Capital, Llc Failure To Obey The Court'S Jan. 20, 2011 And Feb. 14, 2011 Orders And For Discovery Abuses

rulings.Case Title: TITAN SERVICES INC., AN ARIZONA CORPORATION VS. PHOENIX ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 15, 2011, line 5, PLAINTIFF TITAN SERVICES INC., AN ARIZONA CORPORATION Notice And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment. PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH AND TO ISSUE A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $258,521.75 (AMOUNT OWED + ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS). UNDISPUTED FACTS ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF SHOW THAT A SUBCONTRACT WAS EXECUTED BETWEEN THE PARTIES AND THE DEFENDANT FAILED TO PAY. FURTHER, WHILE DEFENDANT CLAIMS THAT THERE SHOULD BE AN OFFSET, DEFENDANT OFFERS NO EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THIS CLAIM. FINALLY, DEFENDANT FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE, OR EVEN CLAIMS THAT IT WOULD BE PERSONALLY HARMED BY ISSUANCE OF A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IN THIS CASE. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS REGARDING IMPROPER SERVICE ARE UNPERSUASIVE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: RANDY KENNISON, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS VS. ALLSCRIPTSMISYS LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Proceedinggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 15, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT ALLSCRIPTSMISYS LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Proceedings. MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND FOR A STAY OF PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: RANDY KENNISON, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS VS. ALLSCRIPTSMISYS LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motionand Joinder Of Arbitration Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 15, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFF RANDY KENNISON, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS MICROMAGIC BUSINESS SYSTEMS Motion and Joinder Of Arbitration Proceedings. MOTION FOR JOINDER OF THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS TO THIS ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANTS ARE PARTNERS THAT CAN BIND EACH OTHER UNDER A SEQUENTIAL CONTRACT THEORY. ALSO, BOTH DEFENDANTS ARE WILLING TO ARBITRATE THIS MATTER. =(302/LMG)
